steps.title: Check current dex deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dex version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dex
helm list -n dex 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dex installation.

steps.title: Review dex configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dex configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dex
kubectl get configmap -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dex
```
I am currently trying to get dex working in a ruby environment. On the RP side I am currently working with [omniauth-openid-connect](https://github.com/jjbohn/omniauth-openid-connect), which has some issues of its own.

steps.title: Apply the fix for UserInfo endpoint
steps.description:
Dex added a UserInfo endpoint implementation in PR #1473, providing an OIDC-compliant /userinfo endpoint that returns claims about the authenticated user. This enables standard OIDC clients to retrieve user profile information after authentication.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dex
kubectl get events -n dex --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "UserInfo endpoint" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Dex added a UserInfo endpoint implementation in PR #1473, providing an OIDC-compliant /userinfo endpoint that returns claims about the authenticated user. This enables standard OIDC clients to retrieve user profile information after authentication.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dex installed or the issue environment reproducible.
